evals.prompt: I need to design a payments modernisation architecture for a mid-size European bank. They currently run a legacy SWIFT/SEPA payment hub from 2008. They want to move to real-time payments (SEPA Inst, TARGET2 compliance) and open banking (PSD2). Can you give me an architecture diagram and a brief solution design covering the key components, BIAN domains, and integration approach? We'll present this to their CTO next week.
evals.expected_output: Architecture diagram (HTML) + solution design covering BIAN domain mapping, integration patterns, ADRs, and SEPA Instant handling
expectations: Identifies and names at least 3 relevant BIAN Service Domains
Recommends an event-driven pattern for payment notifications or async processing
Includes an architecture diagram or structured component breakdown
Addresses PSD2 or open banking API exposure
Documents at least one Architecture Decision Record
Mentions SEPA Instant or real-time payment handling specifically

evals.prompt: We're responding to an RFP from a Gulf bank (UAE) for a digital lending transformation. The bank wants end-to-end loan origination — retail and SME — with straight-through processing, credit scoring integration, and a mobile-first borrower experience. I need the technical architecture section of our response: what components we'd deploy, which BIAN domains are covered, and how we'd integrate with their existing T24 core banking system.
evals.expected_output: RFP-ready technical architecture section with BIAN domain mapping, T24 integration approach, STP targets, mobile architecture
expectations: Maps requirements to BIAN lending domains (Consumer Loan, Credit Management, or Collateral Management at minimum)
Covers both retail and SME origination in scope
Structures the response in a format suitable for an RFP technical section
Mentions straight-through processing (STP) and automation targets

evals.prompt: I just had a client meeting and they showed me this enterprise core banking architecture diagram. I want to understand what BIAN service domains are covered versus missing, and whether there are any architectural gaps I should flag before the next meeting. The diagram shows: Channels (Mobile, Web, Branch, ATM), Edge Security (WAF, API Gateway, OAuth), Application Services (Customer Onboarding/KYC, Account Management, Deposits, Loans, Payments, Cards, Trade Finance, Fee Management, Interest Pricing, Alerts, Customer Comms), Integration Layer (ESB, Kafka, Event Streaming, API Management), Core Banking Engine with ACID-compliant DB, Data Layer (Data Lake, DW, Cache, Search), Infrastructure (K8s, Docker, Service Mesh, CI/CD).
evals.expected_output: Structured gap analysis: BIAN domains present vs missing, severity ratings, architectural observations, client questions
expectations: Maps diagram components to named BIAN Service Domains
Identifies at least 3 BIAN domains that are missing or unclear
Flags risk/compliance domains (AML, Fraud Detection, or Regulatory Reporting) as gaps
Provides specific, actionable questions to ask the client
